content.text:
<plugin_info kind="matched_installed">
display_name: Figma
description: Plugin that includes the Figma MCP server and Skills for common workflows
skills:
  - figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
  - figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
  - figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
  - figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
  - figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
  - figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
  - figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
  - figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
mcp_servers:
  - figma
</plugin_info>

content.text:
Here is what was wrong and what we changed.

### Figma (`1:230` — Rectangle 28)
From file metadata and Dev Mode output, the track is **174×44** pt (not 278×46), with **10px** corners (`rounded-[10px]`), fill **`#242c3b`**.

### Code updates
1. **`constants/theme.ts`** — `CHECKOUT_TRACK_WIDTH_PT` is **174**; added **`CHECKOUT_TRACK_HEIGHT_PT = 44`** so width/height stay aligned with the file.
2. **`components/CheckoutSlider.tsx`**
   - Track **`borderRadius`** was `TRACK_H / 2` (~pill). It is now **`RADIUS.md` (10)**, same as the thumb, so the mask from **`overflow: 'hidden'`** matches the thumb’s corners and does not clip them.
   - Thumb is **`TRACK_H - 2 * THUMB_PAD`** (38×38 with 3pt inset) so it sits cleanly inside the 44pt-tall track with the same corner radius.
   - Track background uses **`COLORS.bg`** (`#242C3B`) to match the rectangle fill.

The slider reads as narrower because the **width** matches **174 @ 390** and the **height** is **44** instead of 46, with **rounded-10** geometry instead of a full capsule.

[REDACTED]

</user_query>

[REDACTED]

Making the thumb slightly taller than the 44pt track (46pt) so it sits proud vertically while keeping Figma’s border, gradient, and chevron. Refactoring layout so the thumb isn’t clipped by the track’s overflow.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here is what was implemented.

### Figma (`1:233` Rectangle 29 + `1:234` chevron)
- **44×44** thumb, **10px** corners, **1px white** border, **152.72°** linear gradient (`#34C8E8` → `#4E4AF2` with stops ~1.7% / 100%).
- **`chevron.forward`**: **28×28**, centered with **8pt** inset on each side in a 44pt thumb.

### “A little larger than the track”
In the file, the track and thumb are both **44pt** tall; to match your wording, **`CHECKOUT_THUMB_SIZE_PT` is set to 46** so the handle is slightly taller/wider than the **44pt** track and sits a bit proud vertically (`track` is centered in the taller slot).

### Code changes
1. **`constants/theme.ts`** — `CHECKOUT_THUMB_SIZE_PT` (46), `CHECKOUT_THUMB_CHEVRON_PT` (28), `CHECKOUT_THUMB_GRADIENT_ANGLE_DEG` (152.72…).
2. **`components/icons/ChevronForwardIcon.tsx`** — SF-style forward chevron (stroke, round caps/joins), default **28**; exported from **`components/icons/index.ts`**.
3. **`components/CheckoutSlider.tsx`**
   - **Layout**: `slideFrame` wraps a **track** layer (absolute, vertically centered) and a **thumb** sibling so the thumb is **not** clipped by the track’s `overflow: 'hidden'`.
   - **Thumb**: white border (`borderWidth: scale`), **`LinearGradient`** with Figma angle via `cssLinearGradientToUnitPoints`, **`locations={[0.017011, 1]}`**, inner radius = outer radius − border.
   - **Label**: `left` uses **`CHECKOUT_LABEL_LEFT_PT = 67`** (Figma checkout text at 175 vs frame at 108).
   - **Scaling**: track height, thumb size, chevron, radii, and border scale with **`width / 390`**.

If you want the thumb **strictly 44×44** like the rectangle in Figma (no overspill), set `CHECKOUT_THUMB_SIZE_PT` to **44** in `theme.ts`; everything else can stay as-is.

[REDACTED]

Here is what changed:

- **Removed** the thumb’s **white border** (`borderWidth` / `borderColor` on `thumbOuter`).
- **Gradient** now uses the **same corner radius** as the thumb shell (`r`), since there is no inset border anymore.
- **Size** is unchanged: **`CHECKOUT_THUMB_SIZE_PT` (46)** vs **44** track height, so it still reads larger than the track.
- Dropped the unused **`Text`** import and refreshed the file comment.

input.description: Typecheck after theme and cart changes

input.new_string:
primary:      '#242C3B',
        'inset-well': '#1D2330',
        'card-mid':   '#2B3445',
input.old_string:

content.text:
Here is what was implemented.

### Theme (`constants/theme.ts`)
- **`COLORS.insetWell`** — `#1D2330`, a bit darker than **`COLORS.bg`** (`#242C3B`).
- **`INSET_DEPTH`** — rgba stops for a **fake inset shadow** (top + bottom gradient bands).
- Removed **`inputBg`**; the promo field uses **`insetWell`** like the checkout track.

### Shared overlay (`components/InsetDepthOverlays.tsx`)
- Two **`LinearGradient`** layers (darkening toward the **top** and **bottom**) with `pointerEvents="none"`, clipped with **`overflow: 'hidden'`** and optional **`style`** (e.g. border radius) so it matches each well.

### Checkout track (`components/CheckoutSlider.tsx`)
- Fill is **`COLORS.insetWell`**.
- **`InsetDepthOverlays`** sits under the labels (`labelSlot` has **`zIndex: 1`**).
- **Outer** drop shadow / elevation on the track were removed so depth reads as **inset**, not a raised card.

### Promo row (`app/cart.tsx`)
- Coupon shell: **`insetWell`**, **`position: 'relative'`**, **`overflow: 'hidden'`**.
- **`InsetDepthOverlays`** with the same left-corner radii as the field.
- **`TextInput`** gets **`zIndex: 1`** so it stays above the overlays.
- Previous **outer** “neumorphic” shadow on the coupon field was removed.

### Tailwind (`tailwind.config.js`)
- Added **`inset-well`** so it stays aligned with `COLORS.insetWell` if you use NativeWind later.

You can tweak strength by editing **`INSET_DEPTH`** in `theme.ts`.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what changed in `CheckoutSlider.tsx`:

### Layout
- Labels sit in a **full-width** `trackLabelArea` (`absoluteFill`) so alignment is relative to the whole track.
- **“Checkout”** uses `textAlign: 'right'` and `paddingRight: LABEL_SIDE_MARGIN_PT * scale` (14pt @ 390, scaled).
- **“Done!”** uses `textAlign: 'left'` and the same inset on the left (`paddingLeft`).

### Styling
- **`trackLabelBase`** holds shared typography: `FONT.medium`, `fontSize` 15, **`COLORS.textLo`** for both strings (no accent color on “Done!”).
- **`checkoutLabel` / `doneLabel`** only set horizontal alignment.

### Content & motion
- Copy is **`Done!`** only (no **✓**).
- Removed the **horizontal translate** on the “Checkout” opacity animation so it stays cleanly right-aligned while fading.

The old `CHECKOUT_LABEL_LEFT_PT` inset is gone; margins are symmetric **14pt** from the track edges at the design baseline.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what was removed:

### `components/BottomTabBar.tsx`
- Removed the **home indicator** block (the centered white pill `View`).
- Removed **`HOME_H` / `HOME_W` / `HOME_PH` / `HOME_BOTTOM`** and the **`homeArea` / `homeBar`** styles.
- Adjusted the file header and the outer wrapper comment so they no longer refer to a Figma home-indicator frame.

Tab bar height is unchanged: **`layoutH`** still uses **`barH + insets.bottom`**, so safe-area padding for the real system home indicator is unchanged—only the fake bar is gone.

### `app/cart.tsx`
- Removed the extra **`<View style={styles.homeIndicator} />`** under the checkout slider (same kind of pill).
- Removed the **`homeIndicator`** style.

The OS can show the actual home indicator on its own layer without a duplicate in the UI.

[REDACTED]
